classDescription: An inventive engineer who solves problems with devices, formulae, deployables, and battlefield improvisation.
roleIdentity: Intelligence-based half-caster who mixes support, control, utility, and deployable gadget play.

tinker-tinkercraft.name: Tinkercraft
tinker-tinkercraft.summary: You gain proficiency with Tinker's Tools. During a Long Rest, you can craft Tiny clockwork devices that produce light, noise, music, or simple mechanical effects.
tinker-tinkercraft.description:
You have a knack for building clever devices from scraps and components. You gain proficiency with Tinker's Tools (if not already proficient).

During a Long Rest, you can use Tinker's Tools to craft any number of Tiny clockwork devices. Each device ceases to function after 24 hours, or when you dismantle it (no action required). You can have no more than three such devices active at one time.

When you create a device, choose one of the following options:

**Clockwork Toy.** A moving figure (animal, monster, or humanoid) that moves 5 feet in a random direction each round.

**Fire Starter.** Produces a miniature flame; acts as a tindertwig. Can light candles, torches, or campfires.

**Music Box.** Plays a single preprogrammed song at a moderate volume when opened.

You can also use Tinker's Tools during Short Rests to repair damaged mechanical devices or constructs, restoring 1d6 + Intelligence modifier Hit Points.

tinker-prototype-rig.name: Prototype Rig
tinker-prototype-rig.summary: You construct a wearable Prototype Rig that serves as your spellcasting focus and powers your class features via Gadget Charges. Regain all charges on a Long Rest.
tinker-prototype-rig.description:
You construct a Prototype Rig — a wearable mechanical apparatus that serves as both your spellcasting focus and the engine of your engineering abilities. The Rig's form is your choice: a gauntlet, a backpack with articulated arms, an alchemical harness, or a collapsible toolkit.

The Rig has a number of Gadget Charges as shown in the Tinker table. You expend Gadget Charges to fuel Quick Deployment, Overclocked Device, Reactive Countermeasure, and other class features. You regain all expended Gadget Charges when you finish a Long Rest.

If the Rig is lost or destroyed, you can rebuild it during a Long Rest using 10 gp worth of components.
tinker-prototype-rig.recovery: Regain all Gadget Charges on a Long Rest.

tinker-spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
tinker-spellcasting.summary: You are an Intelligence-based half-caster. Spell save DC = 8 + proficiency bonus + Intelligence modifier. You know spells from the Tinker list and regain slots on a Long Rest.
tinker-spellcasting.description:
You channel magical energy through your engineered devices and formulae, using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability.

Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier.
Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier.

You know a number of cantrips and spells from the Tinker spell list as shown in the class table. Unlike prepared casters, you know your spells permanently and can't swap them except when you gain a level.

Your Prototype Rig serves as your spellcasting focus. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a Long Rest.

tinker-quick-deployment.name: Quick Deployment
tinker-quick-deployment.summary: As a Bonus Action, expend 1 Gadget Charge to deploy a prepared gadget, activate a Rig module, or swap your active configuration mid-combat.
tinker-quick-deployment.description:
Starting at 2nd level, you can deploy or activate one of your devices with blinding speed. As a Bonus Action, expend 1 Gadget Charge to do one of the following:

- Deploy a gadget you prepared during your last Long Rest, triggering its effect immediately.
- Activate or switch your Prototype Rig's active module (such as changing from an offensive configuration to a defensive one).
- Prime a device so it can be triggered later as a free action this turn.

This feature lets you set up complex interactions without sacrificing your full Action.

tinker-tool-expertise.name: Tool Expertise
tinker-tool-expertise.summary: Your proficiency bonus is doubled for ability checks using any tools you are proficient with (Tinker's Tools, Thieves' Tools, Alchemist's Supplies, and others).
tinker-tool-expertise.description:
Starting at 2nd level, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses a set of tools you are proficient with. This applies to Tinker's Tools, Thieves' Tools, Alchemist's Supplies, and any other tool proficiencies you have.

This is Expertise for tool checks — if you already have Expertise with a specific tool, you instead gain a +2 bonus on those checks.

tinker-field-specialization-subclass.name: Field Specialization
tinker-field-specialization-subclass.summary: At 3rd level, choose a Field Specialization subclass. Your specialization grants features at levels 3, 10, and 15, and defines your engineering identity.
tinker-field-specialization-subclass.description:
At 3rd level, you commit to an engineering Field Specialization — the discipline that defines how your Prototype Rig operates and how you support your party. Your specialization grants features at 3rd level and again at 10th and 15th level.

Available specializations include:

**Artillerist.** You construct a magical cannon that can blast enemies, protect allies, or spray fire. Your Rig becomes an offensive platform.

**Alchemist.** You specialize in alchemical formulae — healing compounds, transmutation brews, and volatile concoctions. Your Rig delivers restorative and condition-curing effects.

**Combat Engineer.** You build barriers, traps, and fortifications that control the battlefield.

**Saboteur.** You excel at infiltration, disabling mechanisms, and delivering precise debilitating strikes.

tinker-specialty-spells.name: Specialty Spells
tinker-specialty-spells.summary: Your Field Specialization grants a list of bonus spells that are always known and don't count against your spells known.
tinker-specialty-spells.description:
Starting at 3rd level, your Field Specialization provides a list of specialty spells — formulae that are perfectly calibrated for your engineering discipline. These spells are always known to you; they don't count against the number of Tinker spells you know.

If a specialty spell isn't on the Tinker spell list, it is still a Tinker spell for you while your specialization grants it.

tinker-ability-score-improvement.name: Ability Score Improvement
tinker-ability-score-improvement.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't raise a score above 20 with this feature. You may instead take a Feat.
tinker-ability-score-improvement.description:
When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat.

tinker-overclocked-device.name: Overclocked Device
tinker-overclocked-device.summary: As an Action, expend 2 Gadget Charges to push one Rig device beyond its limits: +2d6 damage, doubled range, or grant temporary HP equal to your Tinker level + Intelligence modifier.
tinker-overclocked-device.description:
Starting at 5th level, as an Action, you can expend 2 Gadget Charges to push one of your active Rig modules into an overclocked state until the start of your next turn. Choose one of the following effects:

**Damage Amplifier.** The device deals an extra 2d6 damage of its type on its next activation this turn.

**Extended Range.** The device's range doubles until the start of your next turn.

**Protective Burst.** You or a creature you touch gains temporary Hit Points equal to your Tinker level + your Intelligence modifier.

After being overclocked, the device must cool down during a Short or Long Rest before it can be overclocked again.
tinker-overclocked-device.recovery: Regain uses on Short or Long Rest.

tinker-modular-infusions.name: Modular Infusions
tinker-modular-infusions.summary: You can maintain magical infusions on items simultaneously. Infusions grant weapons, armor, or tools magical properties. Infusion slots increase as you level.
tinker-modular-infusions.description:
Starting at 6th level, you can infuse items with magic during a Long Rest. You have a number of Infusion Slots as shown in the Tinker table. During each Long Rest, you can infuse a number of weapons, armor, or tools equal to your Infusion Slots.

An infused item gains a magical enhancement of your choice, such as a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls (for a weapon), a +1 bonus to AC (for armor), or Advantage on checks made with that tool.

An item's infusion ends when you infuse a different item in its place, or when you finish a Long Rest without re-infusing it.

Infusion Slots: 2 at 2nd level, 3 at 5th, 4 at 9th, 5 at 13th, 6 at 17th.

tinker-reactive-countermeasure.name: Reactive Countermeasure
tinker-reactive-countermeasure.summary: When you or a creature within 30 ft is hit by an attack, use your Reaction and expend 1 Gadget Charge to reduce the damage, impose disadvantage on the roll, or blind the attacker.
tinker-reactive-countermeasure.description:
Starting at 7th level, when you or a creature within 30 feet of you is hit by an attack, you can use your Reaction and expend 1 Gadget Charge to deploy a countermeasure. Choose one of the following effects:

**Ablative Shield.** Reduce the triggering attack's damage by 1d10 + your Intelligence modifier (applied after the hit is confirmed).

**Flash Charge.** The attacker must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier) or be Blinded until the end of its next turn.

**Intercept.** You impose disadvantage on the triggering attack roll. Resolve this before the attack deals damage; if it misses, no damage is dealt.
tinker-reactive-countermeasure.recovery: Regain uses on Short or Long Rest.

tinker-improved-rig.name: Improved Rig
tinker-improved-rig.summary: At 9th level, your Prototype Rig improves: Gadget Charges increase by 1, you gain one additional Infusion Slot, and the Rig gains resistance to damage when struck.
tinker-improved-rig.description:
At 9th level, your Prototype Rig receives a major upgrade. You gain the following benefits:

- Your maximum Gadget Charges increase by 1.
- You gain one additional Infusion Slot.
- When your Rig would take damage (such as from a targeted attack or spell), it has resistance to that damage type for 1 minute (no action required to activate).
- The Rig can now hold two active modules simultaneously, up from one.

tinker-subclass-feature.name: Field Specialization Feature
tinker-subclass-feature.summary: At 10th level, your Field Specialization grants its second feature.
tinker-subclass-feature.description: At 10th level, your chosen Field Specialization grants you an additional feature. Consult your subclass for the specific benefit.

tinker-signature-device.name: Signature Device
tinker-signature-device.summary: At 11th level, you build a masterwork Signature Device with 3 charges of its own (recharge at dawn). Choose its type: Turret (ranged attack), Injector (heal or cure), or Scout Drone (reconnaissance).
tinker-signature-device.description:
At 11th level, you complete a Signature Device — a masterwork gadget that represents the pinnacle of your craft. During a Long Rest, you build the device and choose its type:

**Turret.** As a Bonus Action, activate the turret to make one ranged spell attack (range 60 feet) against a target you can see. On a hit, it deals 2d8 + your Intelligence modifier force damage.

**Injector.** As a Bonus Action, the injector automatically administers a compound to a creature within 5 feet of you. The target regains 2d6 + your Intelligence modifier Hit Points, or one disease or condition (Poisoned, Blinded, or Deafened) is cured.

**Scout Drone.** As a Bonus Action, send the drone to any point within 300 feet. For 1 hour, you can see and hear through the drone's sensors (Perception +5). The drone has AC 13 and 1 Hit Point.

The Signature Device has 3 charges and regains all charges at dawn. If destroyed, you can rebuild it during a Long Rest for 50 gp of materials.

tinker-emergency-repairs.name: Emergency Repairs
tinker-emergency-repairs.summary: As an Action, expend 2 Gadget Charges to restore 4d8 + Intelligence modifier HP to a creature within 5 ft. Also stabilizes constructs. Once per Short or Long Rest.
tinker-emergency-repairs.description:
Starting at 13th level, as an Action, you can expend 2 Gadget Charges to perform emergency field repairs on a creature within 5 feet of you (including yourself). The target regains Hit Points equal to 4d8 + your Intelligence modifier.

This feature works on constructs (including mechanical allies and animated objects) regardless of any restrictions that would otherwise prevent magical healing.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
tinker-emergency-repairs.recovery: Regain use on a Short or Long Rest.

tinker-masterwork-infusions.name: Masterwork Infusions
tinker-masterwork-infusions.summary: At 14th level, your infusions improve: +1 weapons become +2, +1 armor becomes +2, and tool infusions grant Expertise instead of Advantage.
tinker-masterwork-infusions.description:
At 14th level, your mastery of infusion magic elevates your work to masterwork quality. All of your active infusions are upgraded:

- Weapons you infuse with a +1 bonus now grant a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls.
- Armor you infuse with a +1 bonus now grants a +2 bonus to AC.
- Tools you infuse to grant Advantage on checks now grant Expertise (double proficiency bonus) instead.

Additionally, your infusions no longer expire when you finish a Long Rest without re-infusing — they remain active until you choose to end them or replace them.

tinker-overcharge.name: Overcharge
tinker-overcharge.summary: As a Bonus Action, expend 3 Gadget Charges to enter Overcharge for 1 minute: all Gadget Charge costs reduced by 1, spell save DC +2, Rig deals +1d8 on all damage. Once per Long Rest.
tinker-overcharge.description:
Starting at 17th level, as a Bonus Action, you can expend 3 Gadget Charges to push your entire Prototype Rig into a full Overcharge state for 1 minute. While overcharged:

- All Gadget Charge costs are reduced by 1 (minimum 1).
- Your spell save DC increases by 2.
- Whenever your Rig would deal damage, it deals an extra 1d8 damage of the same type.
- You have resistance to one damage type of your choice for the duration.

Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
tinker-overcharge.recovery: Regain use on a Long Rest.

tinker-dual-deployment.name: Dual Deployment
tinker-dual-deployment.summary: At 18th level, you can have two Rig modules active simultaneously, and when you use your Reaction for a Rig feature you can also use Quick Deployment as part of the same Reaction.
tinker-dual-deployment.description:
At 18th level, your Rig's architecture allows you to run two modules in parallel without interference. You gain the following benefits:

- You can have two Prototype Rig modules active simultaneously (previously limited to one).
- When you use your Reaction to trigger a Rig feature, you can also activate Quick Deployment as part of that same Reaction at no extra Gadget Charge cost.
- Once per turn, you can use one Gadget Charge feature without spending a Gadget Charge (usable once per Short or Long Rest).

tinker-epic-boon.name: Epic Boon
tinker-epic-boon.summary: At 19th level, you gain an Epic Boon feat. Recommended for Tinkers: Boon of Spell Recall or Boon of Fortitude.
tinker-epic-boon.description:
At 19th level, you gain a special ability called an Epic Boon — a feat available only to characters of 19th level or higher. Choose one Epic Boon feat.

Recommended Epic Boons for Tinkers include the Boon of Spell Recall (cast a 1st-level spell without a slot) or the Boon of Fortitude (increase your Hit Point maximum significantly).

tinker-grand-invention.name: Grand Invention
tinker-grand-invention.summary: At 20th level, you complete a legendary masterwork device. Activate it once per turn to devastate enemies, restore allies, or reshape the battlefield.
tinker-grand-invention.description:
At 20th level, you complete your Grand Invention — a legendary device that represents the culmination of your engineering genius. During a Long Rest, you set the Invention's current function. At the start of each of your turns, you can activate the Grand Invention (no action required) to trigger one of the following:

**Annihilator Beam.** All creatures in a 60-foot line must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier). On a failure, a target takes 8d8 force damage; half on a success.

**Emergency Restoration.** Each friendly creature within 30 feet of you regains Hit Points equal to 10 + your Intelligence modifier.

**Suppression Field.** A 30-foot sphere centered on a point within 60 feet becomes an interference field for 1 minute. Spellcasters in the field must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC 15) to cast spells.

The Grand Invention recharges after a Long Rest.

tinker-ability-score-improvement-8.name: Ability Score Improvement
tinker-ability-score-improvement-8.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't raise a score above 20 with this feature. You may instead take a Feat.
tinker-ability-score-improvement-8.description:
When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat.

tinker-ability-score-improvement-12.name: Ability Score Improvement
tinker-ability-score-improvement-12.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't raise a score above 20 with this feature. You may instead take a Feat.
tinker-ability-score-improvement-12.description:
When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat.

tinker-ability-score-improvement-16.name: Ability Score Improvement
tinker-ability-score-improvement-16.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each. You can't raise a score above 20 with this feature. You may instead take a Feat.
tinker-ability-score-improvement-16.description:
When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a feat.

tinker-subclass-feature-15.name: Field Specialization Feature
tinker-subclass-feature-15.summary: At 15th level, your Field Specialization grants its final feature.
tinker-subclass-feature-15.description: At 15th level, your chosen Field Specialization grants you its final and most powerful feature. Consult your subclass for the specific benefit.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your gadgets, spell attacks, and engineering checks
buildTips.secondaryReason: keeps you upright while you operate in the danger zone
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: you still care about initiative, AC, and precise field work
